data.title: Vistance Networks declares $10.00 special cash dividend
data.description:
• Vistance Networks (VISN) declares $10.00/share special dividend.
  • Payable April 27; for shareholders of record April 17; ex-div April 17.

  • The company will pay the distribution using cash on hand, including proceeds from selling its Connectivity and Cable Solutions business to Amphenol Corporation on January 9, 2026. Due to debt market volatility, it chose cash instead of adding leverage.

  • See VISN Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Shipowners eye escape for 800 Vessels as Hormuz Reopening Looms
data.description:
Shipowners are rushing to understand the fine print of a US-Iran ceasefire that could temporarily unblock the Strait of Hormuz and open an exit for more than 800 vessels trapped in the Persian Gulf, Bloomberg News reported.

The vital waterway has been virtually closed since US and Israeli strikes at the end of February prompted Iran to tighten its control, triggering a global energy-supply crunch. Unable to guarantee the safety of thousands of seafarers and their cargoes, vessels have instead been loitering on either side, and traffic has slowed to a trickle.

Hours before a deadline set by President Donald Trump ran out, the two sides on Tuesday agreed to a ceasefire in exchange for a reopening of the strait. Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for May delivery is currently down 14.4% to $96.64/bbl, and front-month Brent crude (CO1:COM) for June delivery slipped 13% to $95.05 at press time.

For shipowners, however, the news has been enough to prompt cautious optimism. The Japanese Shipowners’ Association, a major industry group, was among those that said it would check the details of the US-Iran agreement before relaying information, the report said.

Most, however, warned that more clarity would be required for ships to move, and even in the best case, flows would take time to resume. In peacetime, some 135 ships transit daily, a figure that has shrunk dramatically, the report added.

“You don’t switch global shipping flows back on in 24 hours,” said Jennifer Parker, adjunct professor at the University of Western Australia Defence and Security Institute. “Tanker owners, insurers and crews need to believe the risk has actually reduced — not just paused.”

Ships that transport energy make up a large part of the fleet that’s stuck inside the gulf, data from Kpler show. There are currently 426 tankers hauling crude oil and clean fuels, plus 34 liquefied petroleum gas carriers and 19 liquefied natural gas vessels. The remainder are carrying dry commodities, like agricultural or metal products, and or containers, Bloomberg reported.

Potentially relevant stocks include Frontline (FRO), Teekay Tankers (TNK), Nordic American Tankers (NAT), DHT Holdings (DHT), Scorpio Tankers (STNG), Okeanis Eco Tankers (ECO), International Seaways (INSW), Tsakos Energy Navigation (TEN), Torm (TRMD), Hafnia (HAFN), CMB.Tech (CMBT), Ardmore Shipping (ASC).

Energy-related ETFs: (USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI)

data.title: AI startup Perplexity’s revenue soars 50%, ARR hits $450M: FT
data.description:
• Perplexity’s revenues have jumped about 50% in a month, with estimated annual recurring revenue rising to more than $450 million in March, the Financial Times reported.
  • The surge in revenue comes after the launch of a new agent tool and a shift to usage-based pricing, the report said.
  • Before the new pricing system, Perplexity had grown its ARR to $305M from $16M over two years. A Perplexity executive told the FT that revenue retention was “strong,” without providing a figure.
  • Nvidia (NVDA) and Jeff Bezos-backed Perplexity AI recently got a boost as a U.S. appeals court paused a California ruling that had blocked the company's AI-powered shopping tool on Amazon (AMZN).

data.title: WD-40 declares $1.02 dividend
data.description:

  • See WDFC Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Gold, silver surge to 3-week highs as Iran ceasefire sends dollar & oil plunging
data.description:
Gold prices advanced in Asian trading on Wednesday after U.S. President Donald Trump and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire to finalize talks on ending the war.

Spot gold (XAUUSD:CUR) rose 1.8% to $4,794.08 per ounce at press time, after gaining as much as 3.1% earlier in the session and building on a 1.2% increase from the previous session. The U.S. dollar index (DXY) fell 0.8% to $98.90.

Trump said in a social-media post that he had agreed to suspend bombing less than two hours before a self-imposed deadline to wipe out Iran’s “whole civilization,” adding that the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz was a key condition for the pause. Iran said safe passage through the strait was “possible” for two weeks.

"Risk appetite has ebbed and flowed as this conflict has persisted," ANZ analysts said in a note. "It has also heightened the prospect that central banks will delay cutting rates or even hike them on the back of elevated energy prices."

Gold, a non-yielding safe-haven asset, tends to benefit in low-rate environments and during times of uncertainty.

Additionally, bullion also received a boost on reports that China has accelerated its gold purchases, the brokerage said. Base metals, meanwhile, were mixed, as investors struggled with conflicting headlines of ceasefire talks and threats of attacks.

Among other precious metals, silver prices (XAGUSD:CUR) rose 4.8% to $76.43 per ounce, while platinum (XPTUSD:CUR) gained 4.4% to $2,032.90. Palladium (XPDUSD:CUR) advanced 5.4% to $1,535.50.

Gold and Gold Mining ETFs: (GLD), (IAU), (SGOL), (OUNZ), (BAR), (GDX), (GDXJ), (NUGT), (RING), and (DUST).

data.title: Wednesday’s Economic Calendar
data.description:
7:00 AM MBA Mortgage Applications

The Mortgage Bankers' Association compiles various mortgage loan indexes. The purchase applications index measures applications at mortgage lenders.

10:30 AM EIA Petroleum Status Report 

The Energy Information Administration (EIA) provides weekly information on petroleum inventories in the U.S., whether produced here or abroad.

1:00 PM 10-Yr Note Auction 

Treasury notes are sold at regularly scheduled public auctions. The competitive bids at these auctions determine the interest rate paid on each Treasury note issue.

2:00 PM FOMC Minutes

Detailing the issues of debate and consensus among policymakers, the Federal Open Market Committee issues minutes of its latest meeting three weeks after the meeting.

data.title: Aehr targets high side of $45M-$50M FY2026 revenue as effective backlog reaches $50.9M
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Aehr Test Systems (AEHR) Q3 fiscal 2026

Management View

  • “We’re very pleased with the strong momentum in our business across multiple market segments highlighted by more than $37 million in quarterly bookings and a book-to-bill ratio exceeding 3.5x.” (President, CEO & Director Gayn Erickson)

  • “Our effective backlog, which includes the backlog of $38.7 million at the end of the fiscal third quarter plus additional bookings received since the end of the quarter, is now over $50 million, a new company record.” (President, CEO & Director Erickson)

  • “We received a $14 million follow-on production order from our lead wafer-level AI accelerator processor customer for multiple new fully automated FOX-XP wafer-level burn-in systems.” (President, CEO & Director Erickson)

  • “We recently announced a major new customer win, a major new silicon photonics customer with an initial order for multiple high-power FOX-XP wafer-level burn-in systems… scheduled to ship in this fiscal fourth quarter ending May 29, ’26.” (President, CEO & Director Erickson)

  • “During the quarter, we announced a key production win with our lead package-level hyperscale customer… selected Aehr for production burn-in of their next-generation significantly-higher-power AI processor with an initial production order of our high-power Sonoma systems.” (President, CEO & Director Erickson)

  • “This quarter, we’ll begin shipping Sonoma systems from one of our current contract manufacturers, adding capacity of more than 20 additional Sonoma systems per month.” (President, CEO & Director Erickson)

  • “The company recognized bookings of $37.2 million in the third quarter of fiscal 2026… At the end of the quarter, our backlog was $38.7 million… our effective backlog… has now grown to a record of $50.9 million.” (CFO, Executive VP of Finance & Secretary Chris Siu)

Outlook

  • “We currently expect full year fiscal ’26 revenue to be on the high side of the $45 million to $50 million range provided last quarter.” (President, CEO & Director Erickson)

  • “For the full year fiscal 2026 ending on May 29, 2026, we currently expect total revenue to be on the high side of the $45 million to $50 million range… and non-GAAP net loss per diluted share to be between negative $0.13 and negative $0.09.” (CFO Siu)

  • “We also expect to return to profitability on a non-GAAP basis in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2026.” (CFO Siu)

  • “We also announced… we’ll be changing our fiscal year from the last Friday of May to the last Friday of June effective after our fiscal year ends on May 29, 2026.” (CFO Siu)

Financial Results

  • “Our third quarter revenue of $10.3 million was in line with internal expectations due to delayed orders… down 44% from $18.3 million in the prior year period.” (CFO Siu)

  • “Contactor revenues… totaled $3 million, representing 29% of total revenue in the third quarter.” (CFO Siu)

  • “Non-GAAP gross margin for the third quarter was 36.5% compared to 42.7% a year ago.” (CFO Siu)

  • “Non-GAAP net loss for the third quarter… was $1.5 million or a loss of $0.05 per diluted share.” (CFO Siu)

  • “We ended the quarter with $37.1 million in cash, cash equivalents and restricted cash… During the third quarter… we raised $10.5 million in gross proceeds… Since the end of Q3, we raised another $19.5 million gross proceeds… we have now fully utilized $40 million available under the ATM.” (CFO Siu)

Q&A

  • Mark Shooter, William Blair: “what percentage of GPUs or ASICs or XPUs do you think are burnt in today?” Gayn Erickson: “we’ve been a little bit surprised at how many devices are not yet doing production burn-in… most ASICs are not burnt-in… maybe half” of AI accelerators “across the wide variety.” (President, CEO & Director Erickson)

  • Mark Shooter, William Blair: decision process for package-level vs wafer-level for hyperscaler, and a potential transition to wafer-level. Gayn Erickson: “They are already on the road map talking about the third device, and they’ve asked us about the DFT to specifically put into the third device because they would like to consider that for wafer level on our FOX systems.” (President, CEO & Director Erickson)

  • Christian Schwab, Craig-Hallum: multiyear range of outcomes and memory entry. Gayn Erickson: “The TAMs are multi-hundreds of millions of dollars for package-level burn-in… Wafer-level burn-in… is even higher… [memory]… could drive orders in fiscal ’27 with ramps in fiscal ’28.” (President, CEO & Director Erickson)

  • Maxwell Michaelis, Lake Street: relative demand for wafer-level vs package-level. Gayn Erickson: “the engagement and the work to get a wafer-level burn-in is definitely harder than package level,” citing learning curves and a “miscommunication on the clock” in an AI benchmark program. (President, CEO & Director Erickson)

  • Larry Chlebina, Chlebina Capital: contract manufacturer timing and ramp. Gayn Erickson: “We think our first products would be ready to ship to customers this quarter through May… ready to go by late summer when we see the Sonoma ramp hitting.” (President, CEO & Director Erickson)

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts: slightly positive, with forward-looking probing on adoption, sizing, and timing; Mark Shooter asked to “help us out with” the “vector space,” while Larry Chlebina pressed on manufacturing readiness and timing for memory-related upside. (Mark Shooter; Larry Chlebina)

  • Management: slightly positive in prepared remarks (record effective backlog and multiple customer wins) and more explanatory in Q&A, describing adoption as “the beginning phases” and framing benchmarks as solvable execution items rather than blockers. (“this is still at the kind of the beginning phases of this”; President, CEO & Director Erickson)

  • Versus last quarter: management kept guidance ranges but shifted tone from “softer than anticipated” revenue (Q2) to “strong momentum” bookings/backlog (Q3), while still acknowledging delays and technical iteration in benchmarks. (“While second quarter revenue was softer than anticipated”; “strong momentum”; President, CEO & Director Erickson)

Quarter-over-quarter comparison

  • “Bookings of $37.2 million” (Q3) contrasted with “bookings of $6.2 million” (Q2), alongside a higher “effective backlog… $50.9 million” (Q3) versus “$18.3 million” (Q2). (CFO Siu)

  • Guidance language stayed consistent on full-year revenue (“high side of the $45 million to $50 million range”), while Q3 added an explicit expectation to “return to profitability… in the fourth quarter” and announced the fiscal year-end change and 1-month transition period. (CFO Siu)

  • Q&A emphasis broadened from Q2’s focus on timing of benchmarks, cannibalization, and booking drivers toward Q3’s focus on burn-in adoption rates, wafer-level vs package-level decisioning, and manufacturing capacity for Sonoma. (President, CEO & Director Erickson)

Risks and Concerns

  • “Our benchmark evaluation program with a top-tier AI processor supplier continues to make good progress, but it’s taking longer than we originally expected,” citing “a technical misunderstanding on the clock configurations” and WaferPak redesign work continuing “over the next several months.” (President, CEO & Director Erickson)

  • “Q3 revenue was… in line with internal expectations due to delayed orders,” with mix-driven pressure on gross margin as WaferPak shipments were lower. (CFO Siu)

  • “This year has been lighter in terms of consumable sales, particularly WaferPaks,” with management describing it as customers having “bought systems ahead of the need” and expecting consumables to normalize over time. (President, CEO & Director Erickson)

Final Takeaway

Management highlighted a sharp step-up in bookings and a record effective backlog, anchored by AI-related wafer-level and package-level burn-in orders (including a $14 million follow-on FOX-XP AI order) and new silicon photonics wins scheduled to ship in fiscal Q4. The company kept full-year revenue expectations at the high end of the prior range while adding an expected non-GAAP return to profitability in Q4, expanded Sonoma manufacturing capacity via a contract manufacturer, and flagged benchmark timing and design iterations (including a clock-configuration issue) as near-term execution items while describing customer interest in wafer-level burn-in as increasing, particularly as AI devices move into higher-cost advanced packages.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Vale accelerates Oman maintenance outages to offset war-related impacts - Bloomberg
data.description:
Vale (VALE) has brought forward a scheduled maintenance shutdown of its two iron ore pellet plants in Oman in an attempt to lessen the potential impacts from the Middle East war, Bloomberg reported Tuesday.

The maintenance shutdowns have been advanced by a few weeks, according to the report, which could not ascertain the length of time the plants would be offline.

Vale’s (VALE) Oman operations' annual production capacity totaled 9M tons of iron ore pellets in 2025, ~29% of the company's overall output.

Even as Iran's blockade of the Strait of Hormuz is preventing deliveries, Vale (VALE) is not yet considering changing its full-year pellet production guidance of 30M-34M tons, according to the report.

data.title: Panama approves processing of stockpiled ore at Cobre Panama mine
data.description:
Franco-Nevada (FNV) up 3.3% post-market Tuesday after saying Panama's government formally approved the removal, processing, and export of stockpiled ore at the Cobre Panama mine that was extracted before the suspension of operations in 2023.

Partner First Quantum Minerals (FQVLF) has estimated the total stockpile at ~38M metric tons of mineralized ore at varying grades, containing ~70K metric tons of recoverable copper.

Franco-Nevada (FNV) estimated that processing of material will result in deliveries to the company of ~23K oz of gold and 265K oz of silver.

"This activity does not constitute a reopening of the mine and will not involve ​any new drilling, blasting, or reactivation of mining operation," First Quantum (FQVLF) said.

Cobre Panama produced 350K tons of copper in 2022; the government aims to decide on the project's long-term future by June.

data.title: Stock futures jump, crude oil slides after Trump suspends Iran attack for two weeks
data.description:
Stock futures rose and crude oil fell late Tuesday after President Trump agreed to suspend the bombing of Iran for a period of two weeks, subject to the opening of the Strait of Hormuz.

Futures tied to the Dow Jones average (INDU) rose more than 700 points, or 1.5%, while S&P 500 (SPX) (SP500) futures and Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) both gained ~1.7%.

Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for May delivery currently down 9.6% to $102.05/bbl, and front-month Brent crude (CO1:COM) for June delivery currently down 5.8% to $103.42/bbl.

In regular trading, front-month Nymex crude closed up 0.5% at $112.95/bbl, its highest settlement value since June 16, 2022, front-month Brent crude fell 0.4% to $109.27/bbl, and front-month Nymex natural gas (NG1:COM) for May delivery gained 2.1% to $2.87/MMBtu.

"I agree to suspend the bombing and attack of Iran for a period of two weeks," Trump posted on Truth Social. "We received a 10-point proposal from Iran and believe it is a workable basis on which to negotiate," although the ceasefire is contingent on Iran agreeing to an opening of the Strait of Hormuz.

There was no confirmation of a deal from the Iranian side so far.

Stocks had bounced off their session lows in the final hour of Tuesday's regular trading, and oil had tilted lower, after Pakistan said diplomacy attempting to reach a peaceful settlement was making progress.

Pakistan Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif asked Trump to extend his deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or risk devastating attacks on its infrastructure by two weeks, and requested Iran to "open the Strait of Hormuz for a corresponding period of two weeks as a goodwill gesture."

Energy-related ETFs: (USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG), (XLE)
